name: Path Laguna de los Caballeros (PR-AV 40) from Navalguijo — round trip in the Parque Regional de la Sierra de Gredos
name_original: Senda Laguna de los Caballeros (PR-AV 40) desde Navalguijo — ida y vuelta en el Parque Regional de la Sierra de Gredos
region_intro: Hiking around Navalonguilla offers access to the Sierra de Gredos Regional and Natural Park, characterized by granite rock formations, glacial cirques, and gorges. The region features diverse landscapes, including ancient oak forests, extensive pine forests, and riverside vegetation along its various gorges and streams. Elevated at over a thousand meters, Navalonguilla provides varied terrain for outdoor activities, with the Garganta de los Caballeros gorge and its clear pools being a notable natural feature. Best hiking trails in Navalonguilla The most popular hiking route is Senda de la Laguna de la Nava (PR-AV 39) desde Nava del Barco — ida y vuelta en el Parque Regional de la Sierra de Gredos, an 11.5 miles (18.5 km) trail that takes 6 hours 55 minutes to complete, leading through the Sierra de Gredos. Another top favourite among local hikers is Laguna de la Nava y Laguna del Barco — circular desde Nava del Barco por PR-AV 39, a difficult 16.8 miles (27.0 km) path. This route explores glacial lagoons and offers expansive mountain views. Local hikers also love the Path Laguna de los Caballeros (PR-AV 40) from Navalguijo — round trip in the Parque Regional de la Sierra de Gredos, a 14.8 miles (23.9 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Gredos, often completed in about 9 hours 46 minutes. Hiking around Navalonguilla is defined by the high peaks of the Sierra de Gredos, glacial formations, and ancient oak and pine forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate forest paths to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Navalonguilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Navalonguilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Navalonguilla?
A: There are over 25 hiking trails around Navalonguilla, offering a variety of experiences from moderate forest paths to challenging mountain ascents within the Sierra de Gredos Regional and Natural Park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Navalonguilla?
A: Hikes in Navalonguilla feature diverse terrain, including granite rock formations, glacial cirques, and gorges. You'll encounter ancient oak forests, extensive pine forests, and rich riverside vegetation along various gorges and streams. The area is elevated at over a thousand meters, providing varied landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Navalonguilla?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate to difficult, there are at least 3 easy hiking trails available around Navalonguilla. These typically involve less elevation gain and shorter distances, perfect for a relaxed stroll.

Q: Where can I find challenging hikes in the Navalonguilla area?
A: For challenging hikes, explore routes that lead to the glacial lagoons and higher peaks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380434800/senda-de-la-laguna-de-la-nava-pr-av-39-desde-nava-del-barco-ida-y-vuelta-en-el-parque-regional-de-la-sierra-de-gredos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de la Laguna de la Nava (PR-AV 39)&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.5-mile trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380438956/path-laguna-de-los-caballeros-pr-av-40-from-navalguijo-round-trip-in-the-parque-regional-de-la-sierra-de-gredos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path Laguna de los Caballeros (PR-AV 40)&lt;/a&gt; is another demanding option, often taking nearly 10 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Navalonguilla?
A: Yes, there are circular routes available. A notable one is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380433195/laguna-de-la-nava-y-laguna-del-barco-circular-desde-nava-del-barco-por-pr-av-39" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Nava y Laguna del Barco — circular desde Nava del Barco por PR-AV 39&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 16.8-mile path exploring glacial lagoons and offering expansive mountain views.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Navalonguilla?
A: Navalonguilla is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2653780" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de los Caballeros&lt;/a&gt;, a glacial lagoon, and the impressive Garganta de los Caballeros gorge. The region also features ancient oak forests, including trees over 500 years old, and prominent peaks like El Cancho (2,274 m).

Q: Are there any waterfalls or swimming spots along the trails?
A: Yes, the Garganta de los Caballeros gorge, which originates from the Laguna de los Caballeros, features numerous clear, attractive pools perfect for bathing in various sections along its path. You can also find &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/874276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Trabuquete&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6472086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devil's Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; nearby.

Q: Can I hike with my family or children in Navalonguilla?
A: Navalonguilla offers a variety of trails, including easier options suitable for families. While many routes are challenging, there are paths through forests and along rivers that are less strenuous and enjoyable for children. Always check the difficulty and length of a specific route before heading out with family.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Navalonguilla?
A: The summer months are ideal for hiking, especially if you plan to visit the glacial Laguna de los Caballeros, where surrounding meadows are often graced by grazing cows. The clear pools in the Garganta de los Caballeros are also perfect for refreshing dips during warmer weather.

Q: Is there parking available for hikers near the trailheads in Navalonguilla?
A: Yes, Navalonguilla and the nearby village of Navalguijo serve as gateways to many trails, and parking is generally available near popular starting points. For specific routes, it's advisable to check the route details for designated parking areas.

Q: What do other hikers say about their experience in Navalonguilla?
A: The hiking routes in Navalonguilla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 80 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning natural landscapes, diverse terrain, and the impressive glacial features of the Sierra de Gredos.

Q: Are there any historical sites or points of interest near the hiking routes?
A: Yes, along the path of the Garganta de los Caballeros, you can find rudimentary remains of an old iron mine, adding a touch of historical intrigue to the natural excursion. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3287205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdecorneja Castle&lt;/a&gt; is a notable historical attraction in the wider area.
tips: Beautiful valley that has the name of garganta de los Caballeros and that is nourished by the course of the river that descends through this western area of the Sierra de Gredos.

The Laguna de los Caballeros is in a beautiful glacial cirque in the western sector of the Sierra de Gredos at an altitude of 2,000 meters, surrounded by slopes shaped by ancient glaciations and granite peaks that seem like untouchable sentinels of eternal stones.
